questions.question: Which of the following is the main purpose of using the 'Ctrl + S' keyboard shortcut while working on an MS PowerPoint presentation?
questions.option_1: To start a slideshow from the beginning
questions.option_2: To share the presentation via email
questions.option_3: To save the current changes made to the file
questions.option_4: To close the presentation window immediately

questions.question: Which of the following is the function of Ctrl + Shift + V in certain applications like Google Docs or web browsers?
questions.option_1: Reverts to the previous page in the browser
questions.option_2: Opens the Find and Replace dialog box
questions.option_4: Saves the document as a new file

questions.question: Which of the following key combinations is primarily used to access the File menu in most legacy and standard Windows applications?
Which of the following memory types is also called solid-state storage?

Which of the following input devices primarily translates physical, real-world movements into digital signals that can be interpreted by a computer system for tasks such as navigation and object manipulation in a graphical environment?
Which keyboard shortcut is typically used to paste copied content?
Which of the following key combinations is used to paste copied content?
In Linux OS, which command is used to rename a file from oldname.txt to newname.txt ?
Which type of memory is non-volatile and is used to store essential system instructions in a computer?
Which of the following statements DOES NOT describe the primary difference between SRAM and DRAM in terms of their internal architecture and performance characteristics?
questions.option_1: SRAM is faster and more expensive, making it suitable for main system memory, while DRAM is slower and cheaper, making it ideal for Cache memory.
questions.option_2: SRAM is much faster because it has a simple design and doesn’t need to refresh data, while DRAM is slower due to the need for periodic refreshes.
questions.option_3: SRAM uses more transistors (typically six per cell), which makes it less dense, while DRAM uses just one transistor and one capacitor per cell, making it more compact.
questions.option_4: SRAM uses more transistors per bit to store data, providing faster access times compared to DRAM.
